summary: The final code still uses os.system() with shell=True (bandit:B605), allowing command injection via the user-controlled operation parameter. The security_fixer loop introduced this finding while attempting to resolve earlier bandit warnings, but never replaced os.system with a safe subprocess call or added operation allowlisting.
suggested_actions: Replace os.system() with subprocess.run([script_path], shell=False) to avoid shell injection.
Add an allowlist for valid operation values (e.g., {'backup', 'cleanup'}) before constructing the script path.
Validate that the operation string matches exactly one of the allowed operations, rejecting any unexpected input.

evidence.description: Starting a process with a shell, possible injection detected, security issue.
